skill.name: managing-contacts
skill.description: Use when using Mailtrap Contacts API or UI to add, update, bulk import, list, segment marketing contacts, custom fields, or custom events. Use when syncing contacts from a CRM or warehouse, or building audiences for campaigns.
skill.summary: Manage Mailtrap marketing contacts via API: create, update, bulk import, segment, and sync from external sources.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: This skill is a pure documentation file (SKILL.md, 110 lines) describing Mailtrap Contacts API endpoints. Static analysis flagged 61 patterns, all of which are false positives: bash code blocks were misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, Mailtrap documentation URLs were flagged as hardcoded URLs, and URL path parameter placeholders like {contact_identifier} and {import_id} triggered path traversal and dynamic import alerts. No executable code, no prompt injection, and no malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: Bash code blocks misidentified as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 28 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across the file. All instances are documentation ```bash``` code blocks showing curl examples for Mailtrap API calls, not actual backtick command execution. These are safe documentation patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Examined all flagged lines - they are bash fenced code blocks (```bash) containing curl command examples, not Ruby backtick execution patterns. Clear false positive from pattern matching on code block delimiters.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as hardcoded endpoints
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 28 URLs as hardcoded network endpoints. These are legitimate Mailtrap API documentation URLs (mailtrap.io) and reference links to Mailtrap docs. All URLs are to the official Mailtrap service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged URLs point to mailtrap.io (the official service) or github.com/mailtrap (official repository). No suspicious or exfiltration endpoints detected. These are API documentation references.

low_findings.title: URL path parameter placeholders flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged {contact_identifier} and {import_id} as path traversal sequences. These are standard URL path parameter placeholders in API documentation, not directory traversal attempts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard REST API documentation uses curly brace placeholders for path parameters. These are not filesystem path traversal sequences (which would contain ../ patterns).

low_findings.title: URL placeholders flagged as dynamic import expressions
low_findings.description: The {import_id} placeholder in API documentation was flagged as a dynamic import() expression. This is a template variable for the Mailtrap bulk import API, not JavaScript dynamic import.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The {import_id} is a standard URL path parameter for polling import job status. Not a JavaScript import() call or code execution pattern.

content.user_title: Manage Mailtrap marketing contacts with API and automation
content.value_statement: Managing contacts across Mailtrap's email marketing platform requires knowledge of many API endpoints for creating, updating, and segmenting contacts. This skill provides ready-to-use curl examples and endpoint references for efficient contact operations and CRM synchronization.
actual_capabilities: Create, update, get, and delete individual contacts via the Mailtrap API
Bulk import up to 50,000 contacts per request using async import jobs
Manage contact lists and segment audiences for marketing campaigns
Define and update custom fields for enriched contact profiles
Fire custom events to trigger automations and track contact behavior
Export contacts for backup or migration purposes
limitations: Documentation-only skill - provides API references and curl examples, not executable code
Requires valid Mailtrap API token and account ID configured in environment variables
Bulk imports are rate-limited to 200 requests per 60 seconds per account
Does not handle marketing suppression lists (those are managed in the sending product)

use_cases.title: CRM to Mailtrap contact synchronization
use_cases.description: Sync contacts from Salesforce, HubSpot, or a data warehouse to Mailtrap for email marketing campaigns. Use the bulk import endpoint to efficiently load large contact databases.

use_cases.title: Automated contact list management
use_cases.description: Build automation workflows that add new signups to Mailtrap contact lists, update custom fields based on user behavior, and trigger custom events for drip campaigns.

use_cases.title: Campaign audience preparation
use_cases.description: Clean contact lists, update consent flags, and build targeted segments before launching email marketing campaigns through Mailtrap's campaign feature.

prompt_templates.title: Create a single contact with custom fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new contact in Mailtrap with email 'jane.doe@example.com', custom fields for first_name 'Jane', last_name 'Doe', and company 'Acme Corp'. Add the contact to list IDs 1 and 2.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new subscriber with enriched profile data to multiple lists

prompt_templates.title: Bulk import contacts from a CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file at /data/newsletter_subscribers.csv with columns: email, first_name, last_name, signup_date. Write a script that reads this file and bulk imports all contacts into Mailtrap using the /contacts/imports endpoint. Include error handling for the 50,000 contact per-request limit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Importing a large subscriber list from a file

prompt_templates.title: Sync contacts from CRM to Mailtrap
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Python function that syncs contacts from a PostgreSQL database to Mailtrap. It should: (1) fetch contacts updated in the last hour, (2) batch them into groups of 10,000, (3) call the Mailtrap bulk import endpoint, (4) poll the import status until complete, (5) log any failures for retry.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a scheduled CRM-to-Mailtrap sync pipeline

prompt_templates.title: Fire custom events for automation triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: When a user completes a purchase in our app, fire a custom event 'PurchaseCompleted' to their Mailtrap contact record with params: order_id, total_amount, and product_category. Show me the curl command to POST to the /contacts/{contact_identifier}/events endpoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Triggering Mailtrap automations from application events

output_examples.input: Create a new contact with email 'newuser@example.com', first name 'New', and last name 'User', assigned to list 5.
output: The skill generates a curl command targeting the Mailtrap Contacts API
Includes Bearer token authentication and JSON Content-Type header
Sends a JSON body with the email, custom fields for first/last name, and list_ids array
Expected response: 201 Created with the new contact object including assigned ID

output_examples.input: I need to import 30,000 contacts from our event registration system into Mailtrap.
output: Recommends using the bulk import endpoint to avoid rate limits
Suggests batching contacts into requests of up to 50,000 (one request is sufficient)
Shows the POST to /contacts/imports with an array of contact objects
Explains the async job pattern and how to poll /contacts/imports/{import_id} for status
Notes the 200 requests/60 seconds rate limit and recommends adding backoff for retry logic

best_practices: Always use the bulk import endpoint for loading more than a few hundred contacts to stay within rate limits
Store MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN and MAILTRAP_ACCOUNT_ID as environment variables, never in code or version control
Poll the import status endpoint after bulk imports to confirm completion before triggering dependent workflows
Reference the Contacts OpenAPI spec for current field names and required parameters before generating request bodies
anti_patterns: Creating contacts one at a time in a loop when bulk import is available - wastes API quota and hits rate limits
Mixing marketing contact management with sending-side suppression logic - they are separate products and APIs
Hardcoding API tokens or account IDs in scripts - use environment variables and follow the authorizing-api-requests skill

faq.question: What is the difference between contact lists and segments?
faq.answer: Lists are explicitly defined groups of contacts you add or remove manually. Segments are dynamic groups based on rules like custom field values or event behavior. Use lists for fixed groups and segments for targeted audiences.

faq.question: How many contacts can I import in a single request?
faq.answer: The bulk import endpoint accepts up to 50,000 contacts per request as an async job. For larger datasets, split into multiple requests and poll the import status for each.

faq.question: Do I need a separate API token for contacts versus sending emails?
faq.answer: Mailtrap uses the same API token for contacts and sending, but account IDs may differ. Use the authorizing-api-requests skill to resolve your account ID and configure tokens correctly.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to manage email suppression lists?
faq.answer: No. Suppressions (hard bounces, complaints, unsubscribes on the sending side) are managed in Mailtrap's sending product, not the marketing contacts product. Use the sending-emails skill for suppression management.

faq.question: What rate limits apply to the Contacts API?
faq.answer: The typical limit is 200 requests per 60 seconds per account. Bulk imports and exports are async jobs that do not count against the synchronous rate limit, but you should still poll status endpoints responsibly.

faq.question: How do custom events differ from custom fields?
faq.answer: Custom fields are persistent properties on a contact (like first_name or membership_level). Custom events are point-in-time actions that trigger automations and carry a payload (like PurchaseCompleted with order details).
